title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
summary: Military historian Sarah Paine outlines a two-thousand-year geopolitical framework dividing continental empires that pursue security through territorial conquest from maritime powers that pursue prosperity through trade. The lecture examines how geography has shaped global power dynamics and examines the behavior of contemporary powers like China and Russia through this lens.
writeup: Military historian Sarah Paine presents a two-thousand-year framework that distinguishes continental empires, which seek security through territorial conquest, from maritime powers, which seek prosperity through trade. The lecture applies this divide to explain how geography shapes global power dynamics and constrains the choices available to states. Paine reads the behavior of contemporary powers, China and Russia, through this continental-versus-maritime lens, tying their strategies to enduring geographic position rather than transient policy.
